title: Wire data quality checks into the orchestrator and make them able to fail — checks are unwired, advisory-only, and the one reconciliation check is dead
description: The DQ enforcement layer exists as artifacts but guards nothing. (1) data_quality_checks.Notebook and pipeline_error_handler.Notebook are NOT activities in orchestrator_pipeline_bronze_to_gold — the pipeline runs bronze copies/dataflows -> bronze-to-silver -> silver-to-gold and stops. (2) Even run manually, no check can halt anything: the framework doc specifies raise DataQualityException for strict checks (data_quality_framework.md:161-163, 260-261, 334-335, 411-412, 492-493) but the implemented notebook contains zero raises in 1,595 lines; the gold notebook hardcodes FAIL_ON_UNMAPPED=False. (3) The only silver-to-gold reconciliation check references silver_procurement.spend_eur — a column that does not exist in silver (spend is computed in gold) — so it throws, is swallowed by try/except, and logs a permanent score-0 'fail' into gold_quality_history every run while reconciling nothing. (4) Coverage gaps: bronze_WGI has zero checks; fact_epi_score has no RI or uniqueness check; no gold fact has a duplicate-key check (which would have caught the task-023 fan-out); the bronze duplicate check key (Date, MaterialName, SupplierName, Quantity) doesn't match the silver merge key, so exactly the rows that break the merge pass the check.
acceptanceCriteria: data_quality_checks notebook added as a pipeline activity after silver-to-gold (dependsOn Succeeded), so quality history is populated every orchestrated run.

A severity model is implemented: checks tagged blocking vs advisory; blocking failures raise at the end of the notebook (single aggregated raise after all checks run, so one failure doesn't hide others) and thereby fail the pipeline activity. Minimum blocking set: schema validation, required-field completeness on procurement, duplicate detection on the silver merge key, referential integrity at 0% tolerance.

Aggregate reconciliation fixed to compare computable quantities: silver-side SUM(quantity * unitpriceeur) vs gold SUM(spend_eur) (or an explicitly justified alternative). It must actually execute — no more swallowed AnalysisException logging score-0 (data_quality_checks:1225,1229-1236).

Bronze duplicate check key aligned with the silver MERGE key chosen in task-024 (currently mismatched: check uses Date/MaterialName/SupplierName/Quantity at data_quality_checks:519-520 vs merge on date/materialname/suppliername).

New checks added: (a) uniqueness of lookup_name in gold_dim_country_lookup and gold_dim_material_lookup; (b) duplicate-grain checks on fact_procurement, fact_supply_share, fact_epi_score; (c) RI check fact_epi_score.country_key/indicator_key -> dims; (d) RI check fact_procurement.date_key -> gold_dim_date; (e) bronze_WGI row count + required fields + schema.
[Erik/Fabric] Pipeline deployed and run end-to-end; a deliberately broken input (e.g. duplicate merge-key rows) fails the run at the DQ activity, and a clean run passes.
Decision recorded in notes: pipeline_error_handler wiring is either included here, spun into its own task, or explicitly deferred (it is currently an unreferenced artifact).
notes: AUDIT-DERIVED (pipeline quality audit 2026-07-13): findings #6, #7, #18 (high tier). Not spec-decomposed — empty drift fingerprints.

EVIDENCE unwired: pipeline-content.json activities list contains only bronzecopy_EUSupplyShares, bronzecopy_GlobalSupplyShares, bronze_WGI, bronze_procurement, bronze_EPI, 'bronze-to-silver data cleaning', 'silver-to-gold'. No DQ, no error handler, and no Warehouse-sync stage (medallion_architecture.md:216 claims one — doc side handled in task-033).

EVIDENCE advisory-only: data_quality_checks.Notebook logs+scores only (log_check_result), zero raise statements; framework doc contract with DataQualityException at data_quality_framework.md sections cited in description. FAIL_ON_UNMAPPED=False at silver-to-gold2:60.

EVIDENCE dead reconciliation: silver_procurement columns are date, materialname, suppliername, quantity, unit, unitpriceeur, headquarterscountry, productioncountry (bronze-to-silver:201-210) — no spend_eur. Check config at data_quality_checks:1224-1226; exception swallowed at 1229-1236 logging 'fail' 0.0. This is the one check that would catch task-023/024 spend drift between layers.
FILE CONFLICT: task-022 (In Progress) also edits pipeline-content.json — coordinate or sequence; do not run in parallel with 022.
ORDERING: it is FINE (desirable) to land this before 023/024 — the new checks should fail against the current bugs, demonstrating they work; expect red until those tasks land.
DESIGN NOTE: keep the aggregated-raise pattern (collect all results, then raise once if any blocking check failed) so gold_quality_history still receives the full picture of a failing run.

TREND CHECK CAVEAT (low priority, fix if touching): validate_historical_trend sums metric_value across heterogeneous metrics (percentages + counts + EUR) between the two most recent refresh_timestamps — the % change is not meaningful; scope it to a single metric_name or drop it.

CARRY-FORWARD from task-024 (delete-insert decision, 2026-07-14): task-024 replaced the silver+gold procurement natural-key MERGE with transaction-grain date-partition delete-insert. There is therefore NO silver 'merge key' collision left to guard. Acceptance criterion 4 here ('align bronze duplicate check key with the silver MERGE key') is now RESCOPED: the bronze duplicate check should target GENUINE data-quality duplicates worth flagging for review (true duplicate transactions), NOT merge-key safety. Update criterion 4's intent accordingly when implementing 026.

CLAUDE HALF DONE 2026-07-14: (1) wired data_quality_checks as a pipeline activity after silver-to-gold (dependsOn Succeeded); 8 activities total, task-022's bronzecopy_GlobalSupplyShares preserved; JSON validates. (2) Severity model = post-hoc (check_name,table_name) in BLOCKING_CHECKS classification + a SINGLE aggregated raise DataQualityException in the FINAL cell (after gold_quality_history persistence + detailed-results table, so a failing run still logs the full picture then fails the activity). Min blocking set: schema, procurement completeness, procurement dup, RI @0%. (3) Reconciliation fixed to SUM(quantity*unitpriceeur) vs SUM(spend_eur), executes for real (no swallow) — classified ADVISORY (see FR-006: gold kg-normalizes quantity but not unitprice, so exact reconciliation only holds under uniform base units; the fan-out it used to guard is caught BLOCKING by the new uniqueness checks). (4) bronze dup rescoped to genuine double-entries (Date/Material/Supplier/Quantity/Unit/UnitPriceEUR), kept blocking. (5) new checks a-e added (lookup_name uniqueness x2; grain on fact_procurement/fact_supply_share/fact_epi_score; RI epi->dims; RI procurement.date_key->dim_date; bronze_WGI rowcount+fields+schema). Also fixed validate_historical_trend to scope to metric_name='dq_overall_score'. Notebook py_compile clean; pipeline json.load clean.

DECISION (criterion 7) — pipeline_error_handler wiring: SPUN INTO ITS OWN TASK (not wired here, not silently deferred). Rationale: it needs a failure-propagation topology decision (on-failure dependencies vs wrapper pipeline) + a notification sink Erik configures (owner both); wiring it into 026 would conflate concerns and expand scope. It remains an unreferenced artifact until a dedicated task is created — SURFACED TO ERIK at batch handoff.

ERIK (Fabric) — remaining acceptance (crit 6): deploy both artifacts; DQ activity runs after silver-to-gold (Succeeded); a blocking failure raises DataQualityException -> activity fails -> pipeline halts; a clean gold build passes. Demo: inject duplicate procurement transactions (identical business signature) -> duplicate_detection@bronze_procurement_transactional blocks the run. EXPECTED RED until task-023/024/025 land — the new blocking checks (lookup/grain uniqueness, epi/date RI) are DESIGNED to fail against current pre-fix data (that's the proof they work); DQ landing before 023/024 is fine/desirable, but do NOT enable it as a hard gate on the live pipeline until the fixes are applied or it will halt every run.

CROSS-REF task-030: implementer flagged (non_blocking) that gold spend_eur is likely inflated for non-kg units (quantity_base kg-normalized but unitprice_eur not converted; unmapped units fall through to raw quantity). This is task-030's scope ('unknown units silently pass through as kg, unitpriceeur semantics unverified'). Once units are confirmed all-kg or unitprice is converted (task-030), promote the reconciliation check here from advisory to blocking. Tracked as FR-006.

FIRST LIVE RUN 2026-07-22 (pipeline 6:41 PM): DQ activity ran for the first time and raised DataQualityException on 2 blocking checks — schema_validation@oem_lh.bronze_epi2024results and grain_uniqueness@oem_lh.fact_supply_share. Everything upstream Succeeded (5 bronze activities, bronze-to-silver 1m52s, silver-to-gold 8m7s). The gate works; both findings are about the check definitions, not upstream crashes.

FINDING 1 — schema_validation@bronze_epi2024results is a FALSE POSITIVE. The contract at data_quality_checks:338 requires columns {iso, country, EPI}. Live bronze_epi2024results is 149 cols x 180 rows with columns code, iso, country, EPI.old, EPI.new, ECO.old, ECO.new, ... — there is no bare 'EPI'. That name only comes into existence in SILVER: bronze-to-silver:79 clean_and_rename() drops every '.old' column and strips the '.new' suffix, so EPI.new -> EPI. The contract was written against the silver shape and applied to the bronze table. Fix: either assert {iso, country, EPI.new} on bronze, or move the assertion to silver_epi2024results. Same latent error may affect the required_field_completeness entry at :444 which also lists 'EPI'. Row-count check passes (180 within 150-250).

FINDING 2 — grain_uniqueness@fact_supply_share is REAL but is not catching what it was designed to catch. It asserts uniqueness on (material_key, stage_key, country_key, year) at 0% tolerance. Two structural reasons it must fail on the new data: (a) year is a CONSTANT — silver-to-gold2:1119 sets F.lit(2023), so it contributes nothing to the grain; (b) silver-to-gold2:1437-1444 deliberately collapses every unmapped material to unknown_material_key and every unmapped country to unknown_country_key ('CRITICAL: This preserves all supply share data for accurate totals'). Any two distinct unmapped rows sharing a stage therefore land on the identical grain. task-022 just swapped the source from the EU file (907 rows) to the Global file (2561 rows), so unmapped materials/countries are near-certain to have increased. The UNKNOWN-preservation strategy and a 0%-tolerance blocking uniqueness check on a grain containing those keys are mutually incompatible by construction — this needs a design decision, not a data fix. Options: exclude UNKNOWN-keyed rows from the check, add a discriminator to the grain, or demote to advisory.
DO NOT enable the DQ gate as a hard pipeline gate until both are resolved — it halts every run. Confirmed empirically: retry=1 means 2 attempts, both failed identically.

FINDING 2 CORRECTED 2026-07-22 with measured data. My earlier note predicted mass grain duplication from the UNKNOWN-key collapse. WRONG ON MAGNITUDE: the actual duplicated-grain count is 2, and unmapped materials are 0 (not many). The MECHANISM was right — 47 of 2561 fact_supply_share rows have an unmapped country and collapse onto unknown_country_key, and where two of those 47 share a material+stage they land on an identical (material_key, stage_key, country_key, year) grain, producing exactly the 2 duplicates. But the scale is trivial, not structural.

FINDING 2 REVISED RECOMMENDATION: do NOT make a design change to BLOCKING_CHECKS. The grain_uniqueness@fact_supply_share failure is a 2-row alias-coverage gap, not an incompatibility between the UNKNOWN-preservation strategy and the check. Fix the 47 unmapped countries (task-023/025 alias territory) and the duplicate grains should go to 0, at which point the check passes as designed with no tolerance change, no grain discriminator, and no demotion to advisory. Re-measure after the alias fix before considering any change to the check itself. This supersedes the three-option design decision framed in the earlier note.

FINDING 2 UPDATE 2026-07-22 20:01: the encoding root-cause fix (commit fe47b69, WINDOWS-1252 on both supply-shares copy activities) removed 45 of the 47 unmapped-country rows. Only Cape Verde and Brasilia remain, and they cannot collide with each other on (material_key, stage_key, country_key, year) unless they share a material+stage. Re-measure duplicated_grains — expected 0, which would make grain_uniqueness@fact_supply_share pass with NO change to BLOCKING_CHECKS, confirming the revised recommendation. If it clears, FINDING 1 (the bronze_epi2024results schema contract asserting the silver-shape column 'EPI') is the ONLY remaining blocker on the DQ gate.

FINDING 1 FIXED 2026-07-22 via Option B (Erik's choice): both EPI contracts moved from bronze_epi2024results to silver_epi2024results, where a bare 'EPI' column genuinely exists (bronze-to-silver:95 selects code, iso, country, EPI). Bronze holds the raw 149-column Yale file whose score columns are 'EPI.old'/'EPI.new'. Option A (patching the bronze contract to 'EPI.new') was rejected because it hardcodes a Yale vintage — .old/.new exist only because of the 2024 methodology revision and will move again.

HAZARD FOUND WHILE APPLYING (would have made a naive find-replace a partial break): the two check sites consume column names differently. validate_schema does dict membership on field.name.lower(), where a dotted name is harmless. validate_required_fields builds F.col(f) at :399, where Spark parses a dot as STRUCT-FIELD NAVIGATION — F.col('EPI.new') would not resolve, and the .alias(f'null_{f}') would produce 'null_EPI.new'. So 'EPI.new' was never usable at the second site regardless of which option was chosen. Documented in the validate_required_fields docstring so the next author does not rediscover it.

ALSO CHANGED: the medallion layer was HARDCODED as "Bronze" inside both validators (4 log_check_result call sites) plus their two dispatch exception handlers, so moving a table would have mislabelled its results in gold_quality_history. Added a layer="Bronze" parameter to validate_schema and validate_required_fields, and a CHECK_LAYER map at :334 that the dispatch loops consult — only silver_epi2024results is listed, everything else keeps the Bronze default. BLOCKING_CHECKS updated to ('schema_validation','oem_lh.silver_epi2024results'); a stale entry there does NOT error, it silently demotes the check to advisory, so the tuple carries a comment saying it must track the contract.

DELIBERATELY LEFT ON BRONZE: the row-count check at :243 (150-250, actual 180) and duplicate_detection on ['iso'] at :572. Both are genuine bronze concerns and neither names a column affected by the .new strip — 'iso' is an original column.
ORDERING IS SAFE: silver_epi2024results is written by bronze-to-silver, which the pipeline runs before data_quality_checks (bronze -> silver -> gold -> DQ), so the table exists when the check fires.

RUN 2026-07-22 20:16 (first with the b0c8689 fixes deployed): blocking failures 2 -> 1. FINDING 1 IS FIXED — schema_validation@silver_epi2024results passes. Only grain_uniqueness@oem_lh.fact_supply_share remains. Because the EPI contract move and the country aliases shipped in the SAME commit, the aliases are provably live too, so the surviving duplicate is NOT Türkiye (encoding-fixed) and NOT Cape Verde/Brasilia (now aliased).

SOURCE RULED OUT: parsed fact_GlobalSupplyShares&t.csv directly with the country aliases applied — zero duplicate (Material, Stage, Country) grains at source. 'Brasilia' appears once (Roundwood/E) with no competing 'Brazil' row for that material+stage, and 'Cape Verde' once (Antimony/P). So aliasing did not manufacture a collision. Two further hypotheses also checked and rejected: 'Titanium' vs 'Titanium metal' (both exist as DISTINCT entries in the material commodity-group map at :722-723, so initcap at :1117 does not merge them), and country-side collapse generally.

NEXT STEP IS MEASUREMENT, NOT THEORY. Three wrong predictions have now been made about this specific duplicate (mass collapse; then 0 after encoding; then 0 after aliases). Run the diagnostic below to name the offending grain and its source rows before proposing any further fix.

ROOT CAUSE OF THE LAST DUPLICATE, measured 2026-07-22 20:37. The failing grain is (Antimony, P, China, 2023) with shares [0.3, 51.8]. Source rows: China 51.8% (t=1.1) and Hong Kong 0.3% (t=1.0). The alias at silver-to-gold2:188 — ("Hong Kong","China",0.85,"territory") — DELIBERATELY folds the territory into its parent, so both rows land on one grain. Not a bug: a rollup that maps N source rows onto one country is structurally incompatible with a 0%-tolerance uniqueness check on a grain containing country_key. Only two territory aliases exist (the other is French Guiana->France, which does not currently collide); Taiwan is deliberately NOT aliased. Blocks the DQ gate; see task-023 for the decision.

issues.description: Working tree also shows fabric/bronze-to-silver.Notebook + fabric/silver-to-gold2.Notebook modified (sibling tasks 023/024/025, same fabric/ dir) — not part of the WGI fix; informational.

task_verification.notes: PASS attempt 2 (owner:both, Claude code/JSON half). Attempt-1 defect fixed: 3 WGI checks retargeted from phantom bronze_WB_ESGCSV to live oem_lh.bronze_WGI with real columns (Country Name/Country Code/Series Name/Percentile Rank 2023); phantom names gone (grep 0 matches, positive control bronze_WGI=7 confirms probe). WGI checks remain advisory (absent from BLOCKING_CHECKS). Regressions clean: aggregated raise DataQualityException runs last + unswallowed; reconciliation SUM(quantity*unitpriceeur) vs SUM(spend_eur) executes (advisory, FR-006); criterion-4 dup rescope intact; checks 5a-5d present; signatures consistent; py_compile clean. Pipeline JSON untouched + correct (8 activities incl. preserved bronzecopy_GlobalSupplyShares + data_quality_checks). runtime_validation=partial: crit 6 (Fabric deploy + broken-vs-clean run) is Erik's half.

verification_history.summary: Core fix correct; criterion 5(e) WGI checks target phantom bronze_WB_ESGCSV (stale docs, FR-007) not live bronze_WGI -> dead-check score-0. Local retarget needed.
WGI checks retargeted to live bronze_WGI (grep 0 phantom matches + positive control); advisory preserved; no regressions; py_compile clean. Fabric deploy/run = Erik.

test_protocol.summary: Erik/Fabric half (criterion 6): deploy both artifacts, confirm the DQ activity gates the pipeline — broken input fails at the DQ activity, clean run passes.

steps.instruction: Deploy pipeline + data_quality_checks notebook; run end-to-end on current pre-fix data.
steps.expected: DQ activity runs after silver-to-gold (Succeeded); gold_quality_history populated. EXPECTED RED until 023/024/025 land (new blocking lookup/grain/RI checks designed to fail pre-fix). Do NOT enable as a hard gate on the live pipeline until those land.

steps.instruction: Inject duplicate procurement transactions (identical full business signature) and run.
steps.expected: duplicate_detection fails -> single aggregated DataQualityException in final cell -> DQ activity FAILS -> pipeline halts; gold_quality_history still received the full run first.
'All blocking checks passed'; activity Succeeds; bronze_WGI's 3 advisory checks execute against the real table (no permanent score-0).

test_protocol.automated_results: Static validation passed (py_compile clean; JSON 8 activities; WGI retarget + advisory confirmed; phantom tables absent via grep+positive-control; regressions intact). Fabric execution = Erik's half.
